cretan_hieroglyphic.source: Ferrara et al. 2022, Appendix 1 (CH 043 → AB 01, A 364/B 12, AB 11)

proto_sinaitic.notes: Proto-Sinaitic dalet (door) shares the door/gate pictograph with CH 043; both encode dental stop

semantic_context.cultic_significance: DA- prefix in da-pu₂-ri-to (labyrinth); d-b-r root (word/bee/sanctuary); da-ku-se-ne (Dakushene, attested name)
semantic_context.nws_connections: Semitic dalet = door; √d-b-r gives dəbōrāh (bee), dābār (word), dəbīr (Holy of Holies)

cretan_hieroglyphic.source: Ferrara et al. 2022, Appendix 1 (CH 025 → AB 04)

semantic_context.cultic_significance: Frequent in administrative and offering texts
semantic_context.nws_connections: Possible connection to Semitic tet; te- prefix common in verbal forms

cretan_hieroglyphic.source: Ferrara et al. 2022, Appendix 1

proto_sinaitic.notes: Both traditions derive open vowel/glottal from sacred animal/object signs; aleph = ox head, CH 042 = labrys

semantic_context.cultic_significance: The labrys is central to Minoan religious iconography; da-pu₂-ri-to-jo (labyrinth) on KN Gg 702
semantic_context.nws_connections: Per Gordon: the primary open vowel in a Semitic reading of Linear A, cognate with the ʾaleph tradition. The sign value /a/ is not disputed; the Semitic substrate interpretation is.

readings.source: Ferrara 2022; CH 007 branched to AB09, AB60, AB73
implications: CH 007 is ancestor of three LA signs (AB09, AB60, AB73)

cretan_hieroglyphic.source: Ferrara et al. 2022, Appendix 1 (CH 007 → AB 09, AB 60, AB 73)

semantic_context.cultic_significance: Tree/branch cult central to Minoan religion; Evans's 'tree and pillar cult'; Athirat's sacred tree
semantic_context.nws_connections: The Asherah pole (ʾashērah) as sacred tree; CH 007 tree pictograph → sibilant connects to NWS sacral tree tradition

cretan_hieroglyphic.source: Ferrara et al. 2022, Appendix 1 (CH 020 → B 43)

linear_b.notes: B 13 = syllabic me; me-ri (honey) on KN Gg 702 spelled syllabically. CH 020 bee pictograph also corresponds to LB B 43 per Ferrara 2022, but B 43 is a syllabic sign, not a bee ideogram. The bee/honey connection is lexical (the word me-ri), not ideographic.

proto_sinaitic.notes: Proto-Sinaitic mem (water) shares /m/ phoneme; the bee→me link is Aegean-internal, not Semitic

semantic_context.cultic_significance: THE key sign for d-b-r etymology. CH 020 bee → LB me-ri (honey) offering to the Lady of the Labyrinth. d-b-r root: dəbōrāh (bee), dābār (word), dəbīr (Holy of Holies). The labyrinth is the sanctuary of bees and words.
semantic_context.nws_connections: Ruth Hestrin (1987): Deborah of Judges 4-5 preserves memory of a Canaanite Goddess Deborah; bee/word/sanctuary triad via √d-b-r

implications: Emphatic/sibilant confusion in Linear A script
Affects root identification for emphatic consonants

cretan_hieroglyphic.source: Ferrara et al. 2022, Appendix 1 (CH 012 → AB 23, A 418VAS)

semantic_context.cultic_significance: Central Minoan religious symbol; bull-leaping, Minotaur, offering tables. CH 012 retains the full bull-head pictograph that linearizes into AB 23
semantic_context.nws_connections: The bull sacrifice tradition shared across Canaanite and Minoan religion; mu- prefix in administrative texts

cretan_hieroglyphic.source: Ferrara et al. 2022, Appendix 1 (CH 031 → AB 27, A 328)

semantic_context.cultic_significance: Liquid sign; re- appears in place names and offering terms
semantic_context.nws_connections: Liquid /r/ shared across all NWS languages; the l/r distinction preserved in LA via AB 76 (ra₂ = la)

cretan_hieroglyphic.source: Ferrara et al. 2022, Appendix 1

proto_sinaitic.notes: Proto-Sinaitic ʿayin (eye) → Greek omicron; CH 008 eye motif shares the ocular pictographic tradition

semantic_context.cultic_significance: The all-seeing eye; divine vision in Minoan religion; ʿayin = 'eye/spring/source' in Semitic
semantic_context.nws_connections: Hebrew ʿayin (עין) = eye/spring; per Gordon §150 a Minoan ʿayin-shift (ʿ+a → ō) is posited. The pictographic eye-motif parallel is typological.

cretan_hieroglyphic.source: Ferrara et al. 2022, Appendix 1 (CH 024 → AB 30)

proto_sinaitic.notes: Proto-Sinaitic nun (snake/fish) → nasal /n/; CH 024 plant form takes different pictographic path to same phoneme. Phonetic parallel only, not pictographic.

semantic_context.cultic_significance: Plant-based offering sign; ni- common in administrative commodity lists
semantic_context.nws_connections: Semitic nun; n-assimilation rule preserved in Minoan: /n/ before consonant assimilates (yintan → yittan)

cretan_hieroglyphic.source: Ferrara et al. 2022, Appendix 1 (CH 019 → AB 31)

semantic_context.cultic_significance: Appears in yasharamu libation formula (JA-SA-SA-RA-ME); sa-sa reduplication for emphasis
semantic_context.nws_connections: Per Gordon (1966): sa- in the libation formula = √y-sh-r 'righteous'; yasharamu = 'righteous offering'. NB: Gordon's Semitic decipherment of Linear A is a minority position not accepted by mainstream Aegean epigraphy.

cretan_hieroglyphic.source: Ferrara et al. 2022, Appendix 1 (CH 028 → AB 09, AB 38)

semantic_context.cultic_significance: Enclosure sign; relates to temenos (sacred precinct) concept
semantic_context.nws_connections: Possible connection to Semitic ḥet (fence/courtyard)

cretan_hieroglyphic.source: Ferrara et al. 2022, Appendix 1 (CH 011 → AB 41, B 32)

semantic_context.cultic_significance: SI-signs common in libation/offering contexts; ritual vessel imagery
semantic_context.nws_connections: Gordon reads si- in compound terms; vessel pictograph connects to libation practice

cretan_hieroglyphic.source: Ferrara et al. 2022, Appendix 1 (CH 010 → AB 53, AB 60, A 310)

semantic_context.cultic_significance: Agricultural sign; grain/barley central to Minoan palace economy and offering lists
semantic_context.nws_connections: Gordon reads GRA/HORD (grain ideogram) alongside syllabic ri- in administrative texts

cretan_hieroglyphic.source: Ferrara et al. 2022, Appendix 1 (CH 037 → AB 54, AB 123)

proto_sinaitic.notes: Proto-Sinaitic waw (hook/peg) and CH 037 (serpent) share the /w/ semivowel but derive from different pictographic traditions. Phonetic parallel, not pictographic.

semantic_context.cultic_significance: Snake-goddess tradition of Minoan Crete; faience snake priestess figurines from Temple Repositories, Knossos
semantic_context.nws_connections: Semitic √n-ḥ-sh (serpent), √s-r-p (seraph/burning serpent); the chthonic serpent-priestess tradition

cretan_hieroglyphic.source: Ferrara et al. 2022, Appendix 1 (CH 038 → AB 56, AB 57)

semantic_context.cultic_significance: Opens the yasharamu libation formula: JA-SA-SA-RA-ME; the most important ritual sequence in Linear A
semantic_context.nws_connections: Per Gordon (1966): JA = /ya/ prefix of 3ms imperfective verb form, √y-sh-r 'to be righteous'. The yasharamu formula (JA-SA-SA-RA-ME) recurs in Linear A libation texts; the Semitic interpretation is Gordon's minority reading.

cretan_hieroglyphic.source: Ferrara et al. 2022, Appendix 1 (CH 035 → AB 58)

semantic_context.cultic_significance: SUS (pig) ideogram attested; animal husbandry records
semantic_context.nws_connections: Akkadian šaḫû 'pig'; the sign's pictographic ancestor survives in the ideographic tradition

cretan_hieroglyphic.source: Ferrara et al. 2022, Appendix 1 (CH 034 → AB 59, A 305, A 356)

proto_sinaitic.notes: Proto-Sinaitic taw (cross-mark) and CH 034 (intersecting lines) share the cross/mark pictograph for dental /t/

semantic_context.cultic_significance: The cross-mark as boundary/territorial sign; taw as signature/seal mark
semantic_context.nws_connections: Semitic taw = mark, sign; the oldest alphabetic letter, from Egyptian hieratic

cretan_hieroglyphic.source: Ferrara et al. 2022, Appendix 1 (CH 009 → AB 61, A 123, A 188)

semantic_context.cultic_significance: O-vowel rarity in Linear A corpus supports non-Greek (Semitic) language substrate — Gordon's key distributional argument
semantic_context.nws_connections: Semitic languages are O-poor; LA O-vowel frequency matches NWS pattern

implications: Affects skeleton extraction for all AB67 occurrences

cretan_hieroglyphic.source: Ferrara et al. 2022, Appendix 1 (CH 002 → AB 70)

proto_sinaitic.notes: Velar/uvular stop; k/g/q merger in Linear A script means CH 002 and qoph share the velar phonetic domain

semantic_context.cultic_significance: Maritime sign; ko- in place names; the thalassocratic Minoan state as naval power
semantic_context.nws_connections: K/G/Q merger in LA script parallels the Semitic emphatic distinction (k vs q vs g) that LA collapses. Per Gordon, this merger is evidence of a Semitic substrate; the scriptological observation (k/g/q written with same signs) is uncontroversial.

cretan_hieroglyphic.source: Ferrara et al. 2022, Appendix 1 (CH 045 → AB 74)

proto_sinaitic.notes: Proto-Sinaitic zayin (weapon) → voiced sibilant /z/; different pictographic path to same phoneme as CH 045

semantic_context.cultic_significance: Paired-vessel motif in Minoan ritual contexts; double offering
semantic_context.nws_connections: Semitic zayin = weapon; the z-series distinction (za/ze/zo vs sa/se/si) mirrors NWS sibilant system

readings.source: Base sign RA; RA2 distinguished as /la/
implications: Affects Semitic cognate matching for liquid consonants
